one_time_description: BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing Limited Partnership one-time distribution of $1,218,004 (nil in prior year)
economic_notes: Village Council owns 50% of Haida Gwaii Forest Products Joint Venture (wood products processing and sale); impairment of joint venture $202,365 in 2020. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event. Health programs transferred to new Society effective April 1, 2020.
notes: Audit is qualified due to cash sales completeness and inability to verify budget approval. Auditor location is Surrey BC but firm name not explicitly stated beyond 'Chartered Professional Accountants'. Three entities fully consolidated: Old Massett Economic Development Corporation, Old Massett Economic Development Limited Partnership, and 1199196 B.C. Ltd. Joint venture (Haida Gwaii Forest Products) accounted for using modified equity method. Correction of error related to housing capitalization restated prior year figures. BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing of $1,218,004 appears non-recurring. Health programs transferred to new Society April 1, 2020 — material subsequent event. Cash is negative (bank indebtedness) at year end. total_government_transfers includes ISC $8,794,741 + FNHA $4,143,407 + Province of BC $1,422,710 + Gwaii Trust $306,113 + Government of Canada $175,327 + TRIC $6,853 + Gaming $1,218,004 = $16,067,155; osr = 17,798,892 - 16,067,155 = 1,731,737.
